title: IS428 Visual Analytics & Business Intelligence Project

yaml.title: IS428 Visual Analytics & Business Intelligence Project

markdown.markdown:
Mini-Challenge 2 presents a visual analytics problem centered on bias, incomplete evidence, and conflicting narratives. Two interest groups, **FILAH** and **TROUT**, accuse the **COOTEFOO** committee of opposing forms of economic bias, yet both rely on partial datasets. This creates an ideal setting for visual analytics because the challenge is not just to inspect data, but to understand how different data samples can produce different stories. Our project helps a non-technical journalist explore these competing claims transparently, identify whether bias exists, and distinguish genuine behavioral patterns from distortions caused by missing or selective evidence. ## Project Objectives The project aims to design an interactive Tableau-based visual analytics dashboard that helps users: - examine whether FILAH's and TROUT's accusations are supported by their own datasets; - compare these partial views against the complete journalist dataset; - analyze how COOTEFOO members spend their time across meetings, discussions, plans, trips, and locations; - identify members whose apparent behavior changes most when the full dataset is considered; - highlight where sampling bias or omitted evidence most affects judgment. The overall goal is to support evidence-based interpretation of committee bias. ## Data The project uses the MC2 knowledge graph datasets: **FILAH.json**, **TROUT.json**, and **journalist.json**. These are built from two main sources: meeting notes and travel records. The graph contains entities such as people, organisations, places, meetings, discussions, plans, topics, and trips, with attributes including date, time, role, sentiment, industry, latitude, and longitude. These datasets are linked through shared person and place records, allowing comparison between partial and complete views of the same committee activities. ## Scope of Work Our scope covers four main components. ### 1. Data Preprocessing and Preparation The JSON knowledge graph datasets (FILAH, TROUT, and journalist datasets) will be preprocessed and transformed into relational tables suitable for analysis in Tableau. This includes extracting nodes and edges, cleaning attributes such as dates, locations, and sentiment values, and structuring the data to enable efficient querying and visualization. ### 2. Dashboard Design and Development Interactive Tableau dashboards will be designed to support multiple analytical perspectives. These include dashboards for committee overview, member-level behavior, travel and location patterns, topic and sentiment analysis, and comparisons between the partial datasets (FILAH and TROUT) and the full journalist dataset. ### 3. Visual Analytical Workflow Development We will develop visual analytic workflows that allow users to interactively explore the data and trace accusations back to the supporting or missing evidence. These workflows will enable users to examine differences between datasets, investigate individual committee members, and identify patterns that may indicate bias or incomplete information. ### 4. Analysis and Interpretation of Findings The final stage will synthesize analytical findings into a clear narrative explaining whether observed bias in COOTEFOO member behavior is genuine, exaggerated, or influenced by sampling limitations. The project will focus on explanation, comparison, and interpretive visual analysis rather than predictive modeling. ## Project Schedule (Gantt Chart)
| Task | Week 1 (12–18 Mar) | Week 2 (19–25 Mar) | Week 3 (26 Mar–1 Apr) | Week 4 (2–8 Apr) | |------|--------------------|--------------------|------------------------|-------------------| | Understand challenge & define research questions | | | | | | Create Project Proposal | | | | | | Data cleaning | | | | | | Exploratory analysis of FILAH, TROUT, journalist datasets | | | | | | Design Dashboard & storyboarding | | | | | | Tableau dashboard development | | | | | | Data Visualisation refinement & comparison analysis | | | | | | Poster & website | | | | |
